title: celtis occidentalis subsp. georgiana
intro: Celtis occidentalis subsp. georgiana, also known as the southern hackberry, is a subspecies of deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southeastern United States. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health. The leaves and bark of the southern hackberry can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
